summary: `examples/base-harness` still authors the retired single-kind `to` spelling — two latent type errors against the SDK's non-empty-set tuple, caught by no gate

edit.description: The two bare-string `to` fields become one-element sets — the lossless spelling of the same declaration, not a semantic change: `edgeFields: [{ field: "implemented-by", to: "source" }]` (135) → `to: ["source"]`, and `edgeFields: [{ field: "superseded-by", to: "decision" }]` (182) → `to: ["decision"]`. Both are type errors against the shipped SDK today: 3db8c25 widened `EdgeField.to` to `readonly [string, ...string[]]` (`sdk/src/kind.ts:53`), and a bare string does not inhabit that tuple. They are latent rather than red because nothing typechecks this tree — the example carries no tsconfig, and `rg` over `tests/`, `src/`, `sdk/`, and `.github/` finds no reference to `examples/` at all. contract.md's `edge` section is the intent the exemplar must teach: a kind declares the field's target as a non-empty set of kinds.

Re-emitted whole, never hand-patched (`specs/model/pipeline.md`, 'The lock': the lock is tool-written whole and emit is its sole producer). The two bare-string rows — `to = "decision"` (320) and `to = "source"` (326) — rewrite canonical as arrays once `kinds.ts` re-spells. This is the canonical rewrite the lock's own upgrade contract owes (a committed bare `to` reads losslessly as the one-element set, the file is never patched, and the next emit rewrites it whole), never a repair of something broken: 3db8c25 verified `check` over this exact lock exits 0 unpatched on the legacy read. Emit needs the workspace SDK built and installed (`pnpm -C sdk install && pnpm -C sdk build`, then `npm -C examples/base-harness/.temper install`), the same bootstrap `CLAUDE.md` states for `.temper/`.

acceptance: `examples/base-harness/.temper/kinds.ts` declares both edge fields' `to` as one-element sets, so the exemplar typechecks against the SDK's `readonly [string, ...string[]]` and teaches the spelling contract.md's `edge` section declares; the re-emitted `lock.toml` carries both `to` rows in canonical array form; `temper check examples/base-harness` exits 0; `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` stay green. No gate enforces the example tree — state that plainly rather than implying CI holds it.
notes: 3db8c25's own disclosure called this 'outside this fence' — but `examples/**` IS inside build's writable fence (`.flume/chain.ts:223`), so it routes as an ordinary entry, not a human `docs(example):` act. Nothing references `examples/` from any gated tree, so this drift is silent by construction and will recur on the next model widening; whether the exemplar earns a gate is a separate question this entry does not answer. scoped at 53519b9.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-derived at 53519b9, which moved this file again. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525-526 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] as the format documents') and 624-625 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-derived at 53519b9 from the 1402 it was last scoped at — 1b9a6af lifted this file's private harness helpers into `tests/common` and rewired it, moving the region) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding (1386-1388) — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 53519b9 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics (dry spec delta, empty inbox). 3db8c25/1b9a6af moved graph.rs and tests/graph.rs without touching the constant or its cites, so every line cite above is re-derived by reading while the claim is unchanged: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` reads 5 at graph.rs:65 under a cite claiming five. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong, the one thing keeping it out of the staleness-rider class. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 53519b9 and holds on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. scoped at b676656.
